title: Lessingianthus rugulosus
taxonomy: Lessingianthus rugulosus is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the larger group of daisy and sunflower plants. Lessingianthus rugulosus is further classified within the subfamily Vernonioideae. The genus Lessingianthus itself is a member of the tribe Vernonieae. The specific classification of Lessingianthus rugulosus provides a clear understanding of its place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Lessingianthus rugulosus, also known as the rough-leaved lessingianthus, is a plant species native to Brazil that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Lessingianthus rugulosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Lessingianthus rugulosus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
